NO json in the string!

it should be: "d", "tenex-edge:$long-session-id" and the session-id should have only a single id, not a stringified json

Plugin is now updated. The fix is in place — new opencode sessions will correctly extract `session_id` from the JSON output and store only the bare `te-*` id as `SID`, not the whole JSON string.

**Root cause:** The binary was updated at some point to print `{"session_id":"te-...","short_code":"..."}` on session-start (to expose the short code), but the installed plugin file at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/tenex-edge.ts` was never re-dropped. The old plugin just did `SID = out.trim()`, so it stored the entire JSON object string as the session id. Every subsequent hook call then registered a new session with that JSON string as the session_id, corrupting the `d` and `session-id` Nostr tags.

**Fix:** The plugin now handles both formats — tries `JSON.parse` first and extracts `.session_id`, falls back to treating the output as a bare string. Existing corrupted sessions (`90fe1f` etc.) will stay until those opencode processes restart.
